mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signal strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined effect of PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 4.0 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which correlates with improved corporate margins and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO > 102 fails to generate positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects robust aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 > 23000 fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS) signals central bank accommodative stance, creating favorable equity valuation conditions for positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined effect of CPIAUCSL > 310 and FEDFUNDS < 5.0 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (rising FEDFUNDS) with persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) create headwinds for equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined effect of FEDFUNDS > 4.5 and CPIAUCSL > 310 fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.0 signals tight labor markets that support wage growth and corporate profit margins, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE < 4.0 fails to generate positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong private demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined effect of RSAFS > 700k and HOUST > 1300 fails to produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases.
